column1:
For all samples, full population testing is appropriate and recommended if it is less work for us and the client and provides better assurance. Guidance was written to sample in case full population is not available or less efficient.

For more information about the Trust Services Criteria required by SOC 2: https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022. (Starting on pg. 14)

column3: Trust Services Criteria (SOC 2 Requirements)
column4: Control Activity Specified by the Service Organization
column5: Type 2 Company Responsibilities

column3:
CC 1.2: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
CC 1.3: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
column4: The company's board of directors has a documented charter that outlines its oversight responsibilities for internal control.
column5:
• Provide the company's board charter, ensure it outlines the board's internal control oversight responsibilities.

If the company has no board, management can provide a document outlining their internal control oversight responsibilities instead.
column8:
Please share a dated document (such as the Board Charter) that outlines the board’s roles and responsibilities, including its oversight of internal control.

Please confirm if the Board of Directors includes independent directors or is composed only of executive management.

column3: CC 1.2: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
column4: The company's board members have sufficient expertise to oversee management's ability to design, implement and operate information security controls. The board engages third-party information security experts and consultants as needed.
column5:
• Provide the company's LinkedIn profiles or CVs of board members, ensure they show the board's work history/expertise, (please provide screenshots where applicable, not links as links don't qualify as stable evidence).
• A complete and accurate listing of personnel who are a part of the board of directors committee.
• Approved documentation on what constitutes sufficient board expertise.

If the company has no board, management can show that they have sufficient expertise to design, implement, and operate information security controls or engage third-party experts. In this case the control description will need to be rephrased to reflect your process, please also inform your auditor.
column8: Please share a list of the Board of Directors’ names along with their LinkedIn profile URLs (or professional bios, if LinkedIn is not available).

column3: CC 1.2: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
column4: The company's board of directors meets at least annually and maintains formal meeting minutes. The board includes directors that are independent of the company.
column5:
• Provide the company's most recent board meeting agenda and meeting minutes, the meeting should include topics such as security.
• Include screenshots of LinkedIn profiles, CVs, or similar to show that the board includes independent members.

If the company has no board, meeting minutes for a management meeting can be shown.
column8: Please share a dated board meeting minute held between July 15, 2024, and July 15, 2025, that shows independent board directors were in attendance.

CC 1.3: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.

column4: The company management has established defined roles and responsibilities to oversee the design and implementation of information security controls.
column5: • Provide the company's approved Information Security Roles and Responsibilities Policy, ensure that the policy defines the roles and responsibilities of personnel who oversee the design and implementation of information security controls.

column3: CC 1.3: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
column4: The company maintains an organizational chart that describes the organizational structure and reporting lines.
column5: • Provide the company's up-to-date organization chart.
column8: Please share a dated organizational chart.

column3:
CC 1.3: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
CC 1.4: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.

CC 2.2: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
CC 5.3: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
column4: Roles and responsibilities for the design, development, implementation, operation, maintenance, and monitoring of information security controls are formally assigned in job descriptions and/or the Roles and Responsibilities policy.
column5:
• Provide the company's approved Information Security Roles and Responsibilities Policy, ensure the policy clearly defines responsibilities for the design, development, implementation, operation, maintenance, and monitoring of information security controls. 

Job descriptions can be provided if roles and responsibilities are not covered in the Information Security Roles and Responsibilities Policy for key security roles.

column3: CC 2.2: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
column4: The company has established a formalized whistleblower policy, and an anonymous communication channel is in place for users to report potential issues or fraud concerns.
column5:
• Provide the company's Information Security Policy (AUP), ensure that it includes a formalized whistleblower policy.
• Provide screenshots of the anonymous communication channel in place.
column8: Please provide a timestamped snapshot of the anonymous communication channel.

column3:
CC 2.2: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
CC 5.1: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
CC 5.2: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
CC 5.3: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
column4: The company's information security policies and procedures are documented and reviewed at least annually.
column5: • Provide the company's information security policies and procedures.

CC 7.3: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
CC 7.4: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
CC 7.5: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
column4: The company has security and privacy incident response policies and procedures that are documented and communicated to authorized users.
column5:
• Provide the company's Incident Response Plan.
• Provide a complete and accurate system generated list showing company personnel and their roles.

column5:
• Provide screenshots showing the company's products and services listing and/or user guides available to customers on the products and services offered, please include screenshots of the descriptions available. 
• Documentation and diagrams which provide sufficient description for internal users to understand and support the functioning of internal control.

column3: CC 2.3: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.

column5: • Provide the company's approved Risk Management Policy, make sure the policy defines the process for identifying risks, type of risks, criticality and rating of each risk, and how to mitigate risks.

column3:

column4: The company has a configuration management procedure in place to ensure that system configurations are deployed consistently throughout the environment.
column5:
• Provide the company's Operations Security Policy, ensure the policy includes processes and procedures to see that system configurations are deployed consistently throughout the environment.
• A copy of the company's of baseline configurations.
• Evidence such as screenshots showing how the company is deploying configurations, i.e. gold images or deployment pipeline checks/validation.

column4: The company has a formal systems development life cycle (SDLC) methodology in place that governs the development, acquisition, implementation, changes (including emergency changes), and maintenance of information systems and related technology requirements.
column5: • Provide the company's Secure Development Policy, ensure that it includes the development, acquisition, implementation, changes (including emergency changes), and maintenance of information systems and related technology requirements.

column3:
CC 5.2: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
CC 6.2: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
CC 6.3: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4:
The company's access control policy documents the requirements for the following access control functions: 
 - adding new users;
 - modifying users; and/or 
 - removing an existing user's access.
column5: • Provide the company's Access Control Policy, ensure it includes requirements for adding new users, modifying users, and/or removing existing user's access.

CC 6.5: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company has formal retention and disposal procedures in place to guide the secure retention and disposal of company and customer data.
column5: • Provide the company's approved Data Management Policy, ensure it includes data disposal and/or destruction procedures to guide the secure retention and disposal of company and customer data.

column3: CC 1.2: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.

column5:
• Provide the company's most recent Board of Directors meeting minutes and agenda, just ensure that the meeting minutes show that the company's board of directors or a relevant subcommittee was briefed by senior management on the state of the company's cybersecurity and privacy risk, and that the board provided feedback and direction to management as needed, (or templates/processes in place if there have not been any).

If you do not have a board, management can provide feedback and direction to relevant teams as needed, or this control may be de-scoped depending on the company and risk.
column8: Please share a meeting minute (with a date on it) held between July 15, 2024, and July 15, 2025, that shows the company discussed cybersecurity and privacy risks.

• A policy or documentation that outlines the company's SLAs for remediating penetration test findings.

column3: CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
column4: The company requires authentication to production datastores to use authorized secure authentication mechanisms, such as unique SSH key.
column5: • Screenshots showing the company's secure authentication mechanisms required to access production datastores, (such as unique SSH keys or MFA). This could be screenshots of configurations to show that such mechanisms are required, or users and whether MFA, SSH keys, or similar in place for each.

column3: CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
column4: The company's datastores housing sensitive customer data are encrypted at rest.
column5: • Screenshots of configurations or datastore status for company datastores housing sensitive customer data to show that encryption at rest is enabled.

column3:
CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
column4: The company's production systems can only be remotely accessed by authorized employees possessing a valid multi-factor authentication (MFA) method.
column5: • Screenshots of configurations or similar to show that production systems can only be remotely accessed by authorized employees possessing a valid multi-factor authentication (MFA) method.

column3:
CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
column4: The company's production systems can only be remotely accessed by authorized employees via an approved encrypted connection.
column5: • Screenshots of systems, tools, or configurations to show that production systems can only be remotely accessed by authorized employees via an approved encrypted connection, (such as VPN, TLS, etc).

column3: CC 6.1: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
column4: The company's network is segmented to prevent unauthorized access to customer data.
column5: • Screenshots of configurations or similar to show network segmentation is deployed.

column3:
CC 6.2: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
CC 6.3: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.
CC 6.4: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorized personnel to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company conducts access reviews at least quarterly for the in-scope system components to help ensure that access is restricted appropriately. Required changes are tracked to completion.
column5: • Provide the company's quarterly detailed access review reports or records of review from during the audit period.

column3:
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
CC 7.2: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.
A 1.3: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
column4: The company uses an intrusion detection system to provide continuous monitoring of the company's network and early detection of potential security breaches.
column5: • Screenshots of the intrusion detection system and its configurations to show that an intrusion detection system is in place to provide continuous monitoring of the company's network and early detection of potential security breaches.

column3:
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
CC 6.7: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company uses secure data transmission protocols to encrypt confidential and sensitive data when transmitted over public networks.
column5: • Provide screenshots to show that secure data transmission protocols have been implemented and configured.

column3: CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
column4: The company reviews its firewall rulesets at least annually. Required changes are tracked to completion.
column5: • A report, detailed records, meeting minutes, or a ticket for the most recent firewall ruleset review. Evidence for this control should include details about the review, including rulesets reviewed, not only that a review was conducted.

column3: CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
column4: The company uses firewalls and configures them to prevent unauthorized access.
column5: In most cases this evidence will be gathered by your auditor and confirmed with you, your auditor will inform you if they cannot gather it.

column3:
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
CC 8.1: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
column4: The company's network and system hardening standards are documented, based on industry best practices, and reviewed at least annually.
column5: • Documented baseline hardening standards for each system and evidence such as records of at least annual review (speak to your auditor if there are a lot or this is hard to show).

column3:
CC 6.6: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
CC 6.8: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity’s objectives.
CC 7.2: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.
CC 7.4: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
CC 8.1: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
column4: The company has infrastructure supporting the service patched as a part of routine maintenance and as a result of identified vulnerabilities to help ensure that servers supporting the service are hardened against security threats.
column5:
• Screenshots or records showing current versions for all in-scope systems to determine if they are in alignment with the company's patching process as defined in the system description.
• Vendor documentation for in-scope systems to show current patches at the time of testing.
• Provide tickets, records, or similar to show that detected vulnerabilities are resolved.

Configurations showing automated patching enabled on relevant systems and current versions against the newest vendor release can also work.

column3: CC 6.7: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company has a mobile device management (MDM) system in place to centrally manage mobile devices supporting the service.
column5:
• A complete and accurate system generated list of assets or similar and enrollment status to show confirmation of MDM deployment on all in-scope assets.
• Evidence such as screenshots of configurations showing the MDM is configured and operating.
column8: Please update the Asset Management Policy to include that the company uses a Mobile Device Management (MDM) system to centrally manage mobile devices supporting the service.

column3: CC 6.8: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company deploys anti-malware technology to environments commonly susceptible to malicious attacks and configures this to be updated routinely, logged, and installed on all relevant systems.
column5:
• Evidence such as MDM records showing AV/AM is deployed on all in-scope components.
• Evidence such as screenshots of configurations or MDM records showing signatures are updated regularly.

column3:
CC 7.4: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
CC 7.5: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
column4: The company tests their incident response plan at least annually.
column5: • Provide a report or meeting minutes to show that an annual test of your incident response process was planned and completed, just ensure that the report or meeting minutes include date and attendees, as well as details around the test conducted, lessons learned, and actions taken as a result of the test.

column3:
CC 1.1: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
CC 1.4: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
column4: The company performs background checks on new employees.
column5: Provide a complete and accurate system generated list of employees hired during the audit period.
column8: Please update the Code of Conduct to state that background checks are required as part of new employee onboarding.

column3: CC 1.1: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
column4: The company requires contractor agreements to include a code of conduct or reference to the company code of conduct.
column5: • A complete and accurate system generated list showing the contractors on-boarded during the audit period.

column3:
CC 1.1: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
CC 1.5: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
column4: The company requires employees to acknowledge a code of conduct at the time of hire. Employees who violate the code of conduct are subject to disciplinary actions in accordance with a disciplinary policy.
column5:
• Provide a complete and accurate system generated list of company employees who were hired during the audit period.
• Provide a copy of the company's Code of Conduct, ensure that the policy includes processes and procedures for violations of the code.

column3: CC 1.1: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
column4: The company requires contractors to sign a confidentiality agreement at the time of engagement.
column5: • A complete and accurate system generated list showing the population of contractors on-boarded during the audit period.

column3: CC 1.1: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
column4: The company requires employees to sign a confidentiality agreement during onboarding.
column5: • A complete and accurate system generated list showing the employees hired during the audit period, (including date of onboarding).

column3:
CC 1.4: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
CC 2.2: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
column4: The company requires employees to complete security awareness training within thirty days of hire and at least annually thereafter.
column5: • A complete and accurate system generated list of company employees, including hire dates.

column3:
CC 5.3: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
CC 7.1: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
CC 8.1: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
column4: The company requires changes to software and infrastructure components of the service to be authorized, formally documented, tested, reviewed, and approved prior to being implemented in the production environment.
column5:
• Provide a complete and accurate system generated list of changes implemented during the audit period.
• Provide a screenshot to show that at least one approval is required to merge to the default branch for a version control repository.

column3:
CC 6.2: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
CC 6.3: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.
CC 6.5: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company completes termination checklists to ensure that access is revoked for terminated employees within SLAs.
column5:
• A complete and accurate system generated list showing the users and contractors terminated during the audit period, (including date of termination).
• A copy of user lists for all environments in scope.

column3: CC 6.4: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorized personnel to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company requires visitors to sign-in, wear a visitor badge, and be escorted by an authorized employee when accessing the data center or secure areas.
column5: • A copy of the company's visitor log covering the audit period.

column3:
CC 6.5: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity’s objectives.
C 1.2: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: The company has electronic media containing confidential information purged or destroyed in accordance with best practices, and certificates of destruction are issued for each device destroyed.
column5:
• A complete and accurate list or records showing all of the assets that were recovered during the audit period to be purged or destroyed.

In some cases, instead of media destruction, the company resorts to data wiping to delete confidential information. In this case, please provide your internal tracker or logs that list all the e-media wiped during the observation period, or similar records. The control description will need to be rephrased to reflect your process, please also inform your auditor.

column3: CC 6.5: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company purges or removes customer data containing confidential information from the application environment, in accordance with best practices, when customers leave the service.
column5:
• Provide the company's Data Management Policy, ensure that it outlines how the company purges or removes relevant customer data in accordance with best practices when customers leave the service.
• A complete and accurate system generated list showing any clients that left during the audit period.

column3:
CC 7.3: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
CC 7.4: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
CC 7.5: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
C 1.2: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: The company's security and privacy incidents are logged, tracked, resolved, and communicated to affected or relevant parties by management according to the company's security incident response policy and procedures.
column5:
• Provide the company's Incident Response Plan, ensure the policy includes logging, tracking, resolving, and communicating incidents.
• A complete and accurate system generated list of the security and privacy incidents that occurred during the audit period if any.

column3: CC 6.4: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorized personnel to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company has processes in place for granting, changing, and terminating physical access to company data centers based on an authorization from control owners.
column5:
• Provide the company's Physical Security Policy, ensure it includes processes for granting, changing, and terminating physical access to company data centers based on an authorization from control owners.
• A complete and accurate system generated list showing the users with data center access, including a list modification date.

column3: CC 2.3: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
column4: The company notifies customers of critical system changes that may affect their processing.
column5:
• Provide evidence of your public release notes/updates if they include critical system changes from during the audit period (please provide screenshots instead of links). 

• If the previous request is unavailable, or critical changes are communicated in some other way, provide a system generated list or records that show all of the critical changes that occurred during the audit period.
• If the company does not perform software development and only focuses on providing services with the involvement of data handling, this control may not be applicable, (please inform your auditor).

column3: CC 6.7: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.
column4: The company encrypts portable and removable media devices when used.
column5:
• Provide the company's Cryptography Policy, ensure it includes processes and procedures around encrypting portable and removable media devices when used.

If the company is not utilizing removable media, the company policies should include that the company prohibits the use of removable media.

column3: C 1.1: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: Procedures are in place to identify and designate confidential information when it is received or created and to determine the period over which the confidential information is to be retained.
column5: • A Data Classification Policy or similar in place to help ensure that confidential data is properly secured and restricted to authorized personnel. Evidence that employees have accepted the policy and that the policy has been approved within a year of the observation period or in accordance with company process. (Typically this is done before the start of the observation period).

column3: C 1.1: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: The company prohibits confidential or sensitive customer data, by policy, from being used or stored in non-production systems/environments.
column5:
• Provide the written policy that prohibits sensitive customer data in non-production environments.
 • Evidence such as system scans or reviews from during the audit period showing that non-production systems do not contain sensitive customer data.
column8: Please update the Data Protection Policy to explicitly state that customer data cannot be used or stored in non-production systems.

column3: C 1.1: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: Procedures are in place to protect confidential information from erasure or destruction during the specified retention period of the information.
column5:
• A Data Classification Policy or similar in place to help ensure that confidential data is properly secured and restricted to authorized personnel. Evidence that employees have accepted the policy and that the policy has been approved within a year of the observation period or in accordance with company process. (Typically this is done before the start of the observation period).
 • Evidence such as screenshots of configurations, access restrictions, encryption, and/or similar to show that the company protects confidential information from erasure or destruction during the specified retention period of the information.

column3: C 1.2: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: Procedures are in place to identify confidential information requiring destruction when the end of the retention period is reached.
column5:
• A Data Retention and Disposal Policy or similar with retention and disposal procedures to guide the secure retention and disposal of company and customer data. Evidence that employees have accepted the policy and evidence that the policy has been approved within a year of the observation period or in accordance with company process. (Typically this is done before the start of the observation period).
 • Evidence such as screenshots of a tool, configurations, and/or similar to show that procedures are in place to identify confidential information requiring destruction when the end of the retention period is reached.

column3: C 1.2: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
column4: Procedures are in place to erase or otherwise destroy confidential information that has been identified for destruction.
column5:
• A Data Retention and Disposal Policy or similar with retention and disposal procedures to guide the secure retention and disposal of company and customer data. Evidence that employees have accepted the policy and evidence that the policy has been approved within a year of the observation period or in accordance with company process. (Typically this is done before the start of the observation period).
 • Evidence such as records confidential information destruction. (Your auditor will work with you to select a sample).

column3: A 1.1: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components (infrastructure, data, and software) to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives.
column4: The company evaluates system capacity on an ongoing basis, and system changes are implemented to help ensure that processing capacity can meet demand.
column5:
• An Information Security Policy or similar with processes to see that an infrastructure monitoring tool is to be utilized to monitor systems, infrastructure, and performance and generate alerts when specific predefined thresholds are met. Evidence that employees have accepted the policies.
 • Evidence such as a screenshot of configurations to show that an infrastructure monitoring tool is utilized to monitor systems, infrastructure, and performance and generates alerts when specific predefined thresholds are met.
column8: Please update the Change Management Policy to state that the company continuously evaluates system capacity and makes changes as needed to meet demand.

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company performs database backups daily. Alerts are configured to notify administrators if replication fails.
column5:
• A Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan or similar with processes to see that the company performs backups of customer data at least daily, where available. Backups are to be retained in accordance with the company Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan.
 • Evidence such as a screenshot to show that this is configured.
 • Evidence such as a screenshot or records to show that backups were retained in accordance with company policy.

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company's databases are replicated to a secondary data center in real-time. Alerts are configured to notify administrators if replication fails.
column5:
• The Vanta automated test can be used to show that databases are replicated to a secondary data center in real-time.
 • Provide a screenshot showing alert configurations to notify administrators if replication fails.

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company performs periodic backups for production data. Data is backed up to a different location than the production system.
column5:
• The Vanta automated test can be used to show that production data backups were completed during the audit period.
 • Evidence such as screenshots or configuration settings showing that backup storage is located separately from production systems.

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company has a multi-location strategy for production environments employed to permit the resumption of operations at other company data centers in the event of loss of a facility.
column5:
• Provide documentation or diagrams showing the locations of different data centers used for production operations.
 • Evidence such as the most recent disaster recovery test results showing that operations can resume at alternate locations.

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company has environmental monitoring devices in place and configured to automatically generate an alert to management for environmental incidents.
column5:
• Provide screenshots of environmental monitoring system configurations showing alert settings.
 • Evidence such as all alert logs or notifications sent to management during environmental incidents if any during the audit period (your auditor will work with you to collect a sample, please confirm if there have been none).

column3: A 1.2: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
column4: The company has maintenance inspections of environmental security measures at the company data centers performed at least annually.
column5: • Provide the most recent annual inspection report or maintenance records showing annual reviews of environmental security measures.
